arms_mandatory_baselines: s1-G claude-CLI pipeline, $0.078/legal record API-equivalent (MEASURED, data/haiku-tier/s1-experiments/s1-report.md): the mint-side null the cached-prefix definer must beat; carried as a frozen constant with its comparability envelope registered (ASM-0607) — the s1 pipeline itself is NOT re-run
arm (a) is the within-run reference arm: identical output form to the baseline pipeline on the new transport, isolating the output-form fork (b) and the thinking fork (c/d) from the transport change

host-model / kernel-as-text nulls: NOT APPLICABLE by design — no consumption measurement, no host model; the consumption-side null family (K-A3 tokenizer-vocabulary extension, trained compressor) stays live downstream and is out of scope (ASM-0600)

dependent_vars.definition: per arm: sum over the arm's calls of usage-fields x the frozen ASM-0601 price table, divided by gate-legal records; THE decision variable (ASM-0602)
dependent_vars.name: usd_per_legal_record

dependent_vars.definition: arm spend / concepts processed; also reported as the derived Batch projection x0.5 (projection only, never measured)
dependent_vars.name: usd_per_concept

dependent_vars.definition: gate-legal records / attempted records (real gates.mjs validators; cannot-formalise and parse-fail reported separately, excluded from the attempted denominator)
dependent_vars.name: gate_pass_attempted

dependent_vars.definition: mean uncached input / cache-read / cache-write / output tokens per call from raw API usage fields (thinking bills as output; thinking overhead read as the d-a and c-b output-token deltas)
dependent_vars.name: token_vector_per_call

dependent_vars.definition: client-measured request-to-complete wall time per call; mean and p90 per arm (TTFT deliberately dropped, ASM-0603)
dependent_vars.name: wall_latency_s

dependent_vars.definition: cumulative mean usd/concept at k in {1,5,10,20,40,60} on the cold-start arms (a,b) plus steady-state marginal usd/concept (last-20-concepts mean) on all arms (ASM-0605)
dependent_vars.name: amortization_curve

levels: a: current framework-G output form (JSON record + minimal frame), thinking off
b: direct-symbolic output (record only, compact serialization, zero prose), thinking off
c: = b with thinking enabled budget_tokens=1024
d: = a with thinking enabled budget_tokens=1024
independent_vars.name: arm

levels: ad (arms a,d share byte-identical system blocks)
bc (arms b,c share byte-identical system blocks; output-form instruction differs from ad)
independent_vars.name: prefix_group

assumptions.claim: scope + honesty caps: mint cost ONLY; necessary-not-sufficient; consumption channel unmeasured; K-A4 unpriced; serves the whole coverage-growth track and survives a K-A2 fire; registered as ASM-0600
price basis: frozen usage->USD table with mandatory preflight re-verification; raw usage always logged for reconversion; registered as ASM-0601
decision rule: lawful-arm floors, PASS at 25% improvement margin, FAIL only at no-improvement; registered as ASM-0602
arms + fork split: four section-2.3 arms in source-fed form; word-only and Batch deltas deferred to A-E1; TTFT dropped; volume-runner repair policy; registered as ASM-0603
sample: 45 seed-pinned band-stratified inventory draws + the 15 s1 overlap anchors; English-only (the only executable fail-closed mint path); registered as ASM-0604
amortization protocol + cache-integrity gate: fixed arm order, two prefix groups, cold-start curves on a/b, steady-state on all arms, >= 90% cache-read-positive; registered as ASM-0605
cross-lingual transfer of mint cost is an unmeasured sizing expectation, load-bearing for nothing, resolved by an A-E1 non-English cell; registered as ASM-0606
baseline comparability: API-equivalent vs API-billed accounting, catalog growth since s1, binomial yield noise — named, margin-bought, binding on any PASS/FAIL citation; registered as ASM-0607

verdict/adoption split: mechanical verdict = economics only; section-2.3 adoption rule + fidelity veto applied at the designer-side assessment; the runner never judges fidelity; registered as ASM-0608
A-E2 pairing frame: sizing statements only against the membership UPPER bound; never net-positive, never achievable savings; registered as ASM-0609

coverage disclosure (mandatory): kernel-expressibility coverage 0.3542 at rung molecules-v0, MEASURED by m0b on one incomplete kernel-v0 instance — NOT general coverage; A-F0 makes no coverage claim and its outputs are quarantined under poc/a-f0/, never promoted to the tier by the run

n_planned.detectable_alternative: at the s1-measured 26% yield, each arm expects ~15-16 legal records from 60 concepts, so the lawfulness floor (>=5 legal) is cleared with margin and usd_per_legal_record is estimable per arm; PASS branch reachable at the N-IOC worked estimate (~$0.042/record <= $0.0585); FAIL branch reachable if per-record cost lands at or above $0.078 (e.g. yield below ~14% at the estimated per-concept cost, or repair-rate blowup); no-lawful-arm degeneracy routes to INCONCLUSIVE via the catch-all, and cache failure routes to INSTRUMENT-INVALID, so neither can fabricate a PASS or FAIL
n_planned.expected_calls: ~450 (60 concepts x 4 arms x ~1.8 calls + 2 calibration calls; hard abort at $10.00 cumulative)
n_planned.instrument_gates: prefix_min_cacheable_valid (both serialized prefixes >= 4096 tokens by count_tokens at staging); cache_integrity_valid (cache_read_input_tokens > 0 on >= 90% of post-first-call requests per prefix group); run_completeness_valid (>= 55/60 concepts per arm); budget_within_cap (<= $10.00 and no abort). Failures are INSTRUMENT-INVALID, never FAIL/PASS (ASM-0605)
n_planned.stage_discipline: freeze BEFORE any API call; stage runner + prompt-manifest (serialized prefix blocks + count_tokens results) + sample-manifest (60 concepts, seed derivation a-f0/1|sample|20260710, fetch pins) -> ops amendment pins the placeholders -> preflight (price-table re-verification against live docs, API-key presence, prefix token counts, 2 calibration calls outside analysis denominators) -> arms a -> d -> b -> c serialized at concurrency 1 -> corpus-pin ops amendment over data/a-f0-mint -> log-append final-phase record -> verdict-gen -> designer-side interpretive assessment (fidelity spot-check on the 15 anchors, N-IOC section-2.3 adoption rule, A-E2 pairing under ASM-0609)

scale_rungs: none (API cost instrument; no host model; no GPU; definer = claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 over the direct Messages API)

endpoints.test: frozen threshold rule (ASM-0602), no distributional test: PASS iff best lawful arm <= $0.05850 (0.75 x the s1-G $0.078 baseline); FAIL iff any lawful arm exists and the minimum lawful usd_per_legal_record >= $0.078; else INCONCLUSIVE; lawful = >= 55/60 concepts processed AND >= 5 legal records; instrument gates override to INSTRUMENT-INVALID
INSTRUMENT gate (never a hypothesis test): cache_read_input_tokens > 0 on >= 90% of post-first-call requests per prefix group; failure means the run measured uncached economics — INSTRUMENT-INVALID
INSTRUMENT gate: both serialized prefixes >= 4096 tokens (the Haiku 4.5 minimum cacheable prefix) measured by count_tokens at staging preflight
INSTRUMENT gate: >= 55/60 concepts processed per arm (transport failures <= 5)
INSTRUMENT gate: cumulative usage x price table <= $10.00 and no runner abort; the hard-cap abort is a budget event, not an economics reading

REPORTED-ONLY deliverable: cold-start cumulative mean usd/concept at k in {1,5,10,20,40,60} (arms a and b; arm b at /analysis/arm/b/cum_usd_per_concept_at_k) plus steady-state marginal usd/concept per arm; consumed by the A-E2 pairing frame (ASM-0609) and A-E1 sizing; carries no verdict weight

REPORTED-ONLY: the maintainer's fork read mechanically — output_form_paradox (arm b cuts output tokens vs a but usd_per_legal_record or latency is >= a), thinking deltas d-a and c-b, thinking_helps_cost; adoption consequences are applied at the designer-side assessment under ASM-0608, never by the runner

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: Binding on ANY verdict of this record and every citation of its numbers. (1) MINT COST ONLY: A-F0 measures the price of PRODUCING gate-legal modelAuthored records — it measures NOTHING about their value in use. The consumption channel (K-A3 tokenizer-vocabulary-extension null, trained-compressor null, 1-token-per-concept delivery, accuracy non-inferiority) is entirely UNMEASURED; K-A4 mappability is UNPRICED (ASM-0560); therefore NO A-F0 outcome — including a PASS at maximum margin — may be quoted as end-to-end viability, achievable savings, or net-positive compression for idea A or any mechanism. Net-positive mint economics is NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT (ASM-0600). (2) PAIRING DISCIPLINE: any pairing against A-E2's selection-side numbers must carry the ASM-0609 frame verbatim — those numbers are MEMBERSHIP UPPER BOUNDS (K-A4 unpriced in every cell, listed-word denominator, weight-arm span mandatory) and the only lawful pairing is a sizing statement, never a net claim. (3) SCOPE: this definer model and pinned call configuration; this prefix composition at the pinned catalog state (mint-manifest 2,673 urns); English source-fed minting on this 60-concept sample; the frozen ASM-0601 price table (raw usage logged for reconversion). Cross-lingual mint cost is UNMEASURED (ASM-0606, expectation only); Batch economics are a x0.5 DERIVED projection, never measured; volume-scale yield beyond n=60 is unmeasured. (4) BASELINE COMPARABILITY (ASM-0607): the $0.078 baseline is API-EQUIVALENT accounting on the claude-CLI transport at the 108-id catalog; A-F0 is API-billed at the grown catalog; transport+serialization differences ARE the treatment; catalog growth moves gate-pass in an unknown direction; n=50-60 yields carry ~+/-11pp binomial noise; the 25% PASS margin exists partly to buy this out and the FAIL branch requires no-improvement so it is not margin-sensitive; no A-F0 number re-measures the s1-G pipeline itself. (5) ADOPTION: which arm configures A-E1 is decided at the designer-side assessment under the N-IOC section-2.3 rule with the fidelity veto (ASM-0608); the mechanical verdict licenses only the economics reading. (6) COVERAGE: kernel-expressibility coverage 0.3542 at rung molecules-v0, MEASURED by m0b on one incomplete kernel-v0 instance — no coverage-general claim; run outputs are quarantined under poc/a-f0/ and never enter the tier by this run. (7) A PASS licenses exactly: proceeding to A-E1 design on this transport (still gated on the A-F0 verdict AND a live selection target AND maintainer spend-ack AND the ASM-0560 K-A4-pricing prerequisite, per ASM-0463/0563) and citing the measured mint price vector within scope. A FAIL is a publishable negative on this transport configuration only.
hypotheses: H-AF0-ECON (primary): the direct Messages-API cached-prefix framework-G definer produces gate-legal modelAuthored records at materially lower cost than the s1-G claude-CLI baseline — best lawful arm usd_per_legal_record <= 0.75 x $0.078 = $0.0585 (the N-IOC section-2.2 worked estimate ~$0.023-0.042 is sizing only, never a premise); the kill-side alternative (no lawful arm below $0.078) is live if repair rates or yield collapse on the new transport

H-AF0-FORK (the maintainer's fork, resolved by measurement): direct-symbolic output (arm b) cuts output tokens versus the current G form (arm a) WITHOUT inverting economics; the paradox shapes — token savings eaten by repair calls, or thinking arms (c/d) buying gate-pass only at a net cost — are detected mechanically as reported-only diagnostics

H-AF0-AMORT: marginal usd/concept declines from the cold-start first call to a cache-dominated steady state (last-20-concepts mean), giving the amortization curve that A-E1 volume decisions and the A-E2 pairing frame consume
kill_criterion_verbatim: K-A1-mint (A-F0 form, frozen; the mint-side kill of N-IOC section 2.5 item 1 at this rung): FAIL fires iff at least one lawful arm exists (>= 55/60 concepts processed AND >= 5 gate-legal records) and the MINIMUM lawful usd_per_legal_record — real API-billed usage fields x the frozen ASM-0601 price table, over the arm's full call log including cache writes and TTL re-writes — is >= $0.078, the s1-G baseline (MEASURED, data/haiku-tier/s1-experiments/s1-report.md sha256 238db0ddfaca614f30886afebfee219430062ed067c9f7d2ded83a489118e5b5). A FAIL means the cached-prefix Messages-API definer is DEAD as an economics play at this configuration: volume minting continues on the existing s1-G pipeline or not at all, and A-E1 does not inherit this transport. A FAIL does NOT kill idea A (selection value is A-E2's number), does NOT kill the coverage-growth track, and does NOT touch the parked idea B. Degeneracy discipline (INSTRUMENT-INVALID, never FAIL/PASS): cache integrity < 90% post-first-call cache-read-positive (measured the wrong mechanism); either prefix < 4096 tokens (silently uncacheable); < 55/60 concepts on any arm; budget abort at the $10.00 hard cap. No-lawful-arm outcomes are INCONCLUSIVE, never FAIL: an arm that cannot produce 5 legal records prices nothing.

artifact_hashes.poc/a-f0/prompt-manifest.json: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:serialized prefix block sets for groups ad and bc + count_tokens results + per-block sha256s, staged and pinned by ops amendment BEFORE any API call
artifact_hashes.poc/a-f0/run-a-f0.py: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:runner staged and pinned by ops amendment BEFORE any API call
artifact_hashes.poc/a-f0/sample-manifest.json: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:the 60 concepts + seed derivation a-f0/1|sample|20260710 + per-item source-fetch pins (URL, revision, sha256), staged and pinned by ops amendment BEFORE any API call

corpus_hashes._recipe: kot-corpus-hash/1: digest = sha256 over the UTF-8 concatenation of one line per regular file under data/<corpus>/ (recursive; symlinks and directories excluded), each line '<sha256-of-file-bytes-hex>  <relpath>\n' with exactly two spaces, relpath POSIX-style ('/'-separated) relative to data/<corpus>/, lines sorted by the UTF-8 byte order of relpath; reference implementation tools/registry/corpus-pin.py
corpus_hashes.a-f0-mint: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:run outputs (run-log.jsonl raw usage + gate outcomes + minted record drafts + sample/prompt manifests) assembled under data/a-f0-mint and pinned by corpus-pin ops amendment AFTER the run and BEFORE the final-phase record

model_revisions._note: no host models, no GPU; the definer is the instrument under price measurement, not a hypothesis-bearing model
model_revisions.definer: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 via the direct Messages API (anthropic Python SDK; SDK version + response model field recorded at preflight and logged in run-log meta); pinned call configuration per arm: max_tokens 2000 (a/b) / 3200 (c/d), thinking absent (a/b) / {type: enabled, budget_tokens: 1024} (c/d), sampling params omitted (API defaults), cache_control ephemeral 5m on the last system block

runner_constraints.authorization: A-F0 ~$5-10 sits inside the maintainer's standing clearance (ASM-0463, signed off in docs/next/io-compression-signoff.md section 3.d; run-after-A-E2 condition satisfied per ASM-0563); usd_cap 10 is the clearance ceiling and the runner hard-aborts at $10.00 cumulative usage x price table; budget.maintainer_signoff is reserved for Tier-5 records and deliberately absent
runner_constraints.harness: poc/a-f0/run-a-f0.py (staged + pinned pre-run; mechanical execution only; any undecided situation is a BOUNDARY STOP back to the designer, never runner improvisation; the runner computes no statistics and judges no fidelity)
runner_constraints.infra: serialized calls, concurrency 1, nice-d local process on the shared box; checkpointed after every call
runner_constraints.preconditions: an Anthropic API key provisioned by the maintainer at run time is a HARD precondition (none exists on the box at design time, checked 2026-07-10); absent key = boundary stop to the maintainer; preflight must also re-verify the ASM-0601 price table against live pricing/caching docs and measure both prefix token counts
runner_constraints.provider: anthropic-messages-api (direct; NO claude-CLI fallback — that would silently revert the pipeline identity under test)

title: a-f0-mint-economics — cached-prefix Haiku definer mint economics (the ASM-0463 A-F0 rung, run after the A-E2 readout per ASM-0563): real-API-billed $/concept, $/legal record, token vector by cache class, wall latency, and the cached-prefix amortization curve for minting concepts into the modelAuthored tier, across the four N-IOC section-2.3 output-form x thinking arms; prices the MINT side of the coverage-growth track ONLY (consumption channel unmeasured; K-A4 unpriced; never quotable as end-to-end compression viability)
